name: Southern Sotho (Sesotho)
notes: Southern Sotho (Sesotho), a Sotho-Tswana (Bantu) language, official in Lesotho and South Africa. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Latin alphabet, heavily digraph- and trigraph-based (kg, kh, tlh, tsh, hl, ny, ng, qh for the click series, ...). There are TWO divergent official orthographies — the Lesotho one (diacritics, ⟨kh⟩, ⟨š⟩) and the South African one (⟨kg⟩, ⟨tsh⟩) — which assign different spellings to the same phonemes, so a single grapheme table would be wrong for one of the two countries; separate st-LS / st-ZA specs are the honest unit of work. A full spec must also model the ejective/aspirated stop contrast, the clicks, and tone (unwritten). Input contract: the orthography variant (Lesotho or South African) must be chosen.
